mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate profit growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between consumer credit expansion and housing starts indicates a shift in demand composition toward durable goods and away from residential investment, which historically precedes equity market corrections due to rising credit risk premiums.
failure_condition: The spread between RSAFS growth and HOUST growth shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns (p > 0.10) across three full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation expectations embedded in CPI data outpace the federal funds rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real rates turn negative, triggering a flight to real assets and equity risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by > 1.5pp
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite negative real rates, or the signal fails to predict 60-day returns better than a random walk (IC < 0.02 over 5 years).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings denominator for S&P 500 constituents, driving positive forward returns over a 5-day trading window as institutional investors rebalance portfolios toward cyclical exposure.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently show no lead-lag relationship with 5-day SPX returns, or the signal generates negative forward returns in > 50% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in nonfarm payroll growth below 100,000 per month signals weakening aggregate demand, prompting algorithmic trading systems to reduce equity exposure and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll misses consistently fail to trigger equity sell-offs, or 60-day forward returns remain positive despite three consecutive months of sub-100k job growth.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production alongside rising unemployment reflects a supply-side bottleneck combined with demand destruction, which compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE shows no incremental predictive power over INDPRO alone, or SPX forward returns are not significantly lower during joint contractions (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for consumer confidence and construction-related earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window as market participants front-run the cyclical upturn.
failure_condition: Housing starts surges fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.01 after adjusting for FEDFUNDS.
